title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Automation and Data Client Systems · Program Defense
summary: A production data release board evaluating a changed source contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for header fields, media types, negotiation, json, and runtime validation, producing representation negotiation and validation boundary. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers representation negotiation and validation boundary. Learners progress…
objectives: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Use Content-Type for enclosed content and Accept for preferred response media ranges with explicit fallback. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
